How we work together

Three pathways, one return

Care

For the helpers and the givers. A place to exhale, receive, and gently remember yourself.

Creativity

Creative expression is the language the soul uses to heal. We use it as a bridge and a way home.

Consciousness

For seekers ready to explore the bigger questions — we journey together as partners, with your inner wisdom leading the way.

Root & Return

Grounded in something ancient

The medicine wheel has long taught that we are whole beings — that healing moves in circles, not straight lines. My work is rooted in this way of knowing: that care, creativity, and consciousness are not separate directions but all are tools to help you along one returning path to your sacred center and to deeper connection to all things.

Ways to work with me

Where would you like to begin?

Caregiver
Caregiver Support

One-on-one sessions for caregivers navigating burnout, grief, and the quiet loss of self that comes from giving so much.

Learn more →
Creative
Creative Expression

Guided sessions using creative expression (arts, writing, music, & movement) to unlock what words alone cannot reach.

Learn more →
Conscious
Conscious Journeying

For seekers ready to explore the bigger questions — we journey together as partners, with your inner wisdom leading the way. I offer presence, reflection, and a safe space for your unfolding.

Learn more →
Community
Group Journeys

Seasonal circles and group experiences for those who grow in community — held with intention and deep care.

Learn more →
“

You don’t have to find yourself. You need only return home to yourself — the path is already within you.

— Kaeli Hansen

The work

Come Back to You

Somewhere underneath the roles you play, the stories you’ve been handed, and the ways you’ve learned to survive — there is a you that has been waiting.

You may have spent years being who you needed to be for everyone else. Managing, adapting, performing some version of yourself that kept things together.

The real work — the work we do here — is to go beneath all of that. To find the root of who you truly are, before the world had opinions about it.

That self is still there…just waiting to be acknowledged, and it’s worth coming home to.

About Kaeli

A lifetime of lived experience

My work brings together a lifetime of lived experience and study across caregiving, creativity, and consciousness.

I’ve spent years supporting individuals through caregiving challenges and addiction recovery in peer-based settings. This work is not abstract — it’s lived, practiced, and continually evolving.
